title: Uruguayan Lamb Tagine
description: A flavorful and kid-friendly Uruguayan dish featuring lamb leg, cooked in a traditional clay tajine vessel, resulting in a buttery and molten texture.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb leg with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in the clay tajine vessel over medium heat.
Sear the lamb leg until browned on all sides.
Add the chopped onion to the vessel and cook until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the dried apricots, prunes, chicken broth, and honey to the vessel.
Cover the vessel and transfer to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce is thickened.
Remove the vessel from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Serve the lamb and sauce hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
